  "findings": [
    "Voltage-rated rubber insulating gloves are of the wrong voltage class for the energized work being performed",
    "Glove testing/inspection date is expired or overdue per OSHA 1910.137 table I-2"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately cease all energized electrical work using these gloves",
    "Remove defective/overdue gloves from service and tag as defective",
    "Obtain properly classed and current-tested voltage gloves before resuming work",
    "Verify glove voltage class against nominal system voltage per NFPA 70E Table 130.7(C)(7)"
  ],
  "rationale": "Using voltage gloves of an incorrect class or with overdue electrical testing exposes workers to arc flash and shock hazards. This is non-compliant with OSHA 1910.137 and NFPA 70E, representing an imminent danger (critical risk).",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.137(a)(1)",
    "OSHA 1910.137(b)(2) and Table I-2",
    "NFPA 70E Section 130.7(C)(7) and Table 130.7(C)(7)(c)"
  ]
}
